operational/configuration reference (env vars, configuration table — the file ADR-1 cites)\n├── README-PHASE4.md         ← Phase 4 verification evidence (cited by Implementation Phases)\n├── config/\n│   ├── registry.yaml        ← project registry (hot-reloaded by router)\n│   ├── clusters.yaml        ← cluster → ArgoCD endpoint mapping (see Fetch Strand)\n│   ├── monitoring.yaml      ← ambient monitoring rules\n│   └── exceptions.yaml      ← exception routing rules\n├── prompts/\n│   ├── router.md            ← intent segmentation prompt\n│   ├── synthesize.md        ← result generation prompt\n│   ├── voice.md             ← voice model system prompt\n│   ├── urgency.md           ← urgency classification prompt\n│   ├── fetch/               ← per-intent-type fetch instructions\n│   │   ├── status.md\n│   │   ├── action.md\n│   │   ├── lookup-logs.md   ← lookup matrices are per lookup_kind (see Intent Router)\n│   │   ├── lookup-config.md\n│   │   ├── lookup-docs.md\n│   │   └── ...\n│   └── escalate/            ← escalate/task-profile prompts\n│       └── task-profile.md\n├── src/\n│   ├── main.py              ← FastAPI app entry point\n│   ├── registry.py          ← project registry loader\n│   ├── agents/              ← agent implementations\n│   │   ├── self_modification.py  ← self-improvement agent\n│   │   └── ui_regen.py          ← UI-regen agent\n│   ├── components/          ← component library and hot-reload\n│   │   ├── library.py           ← component library DB operations\n│   │   └── hot_reload.py        ← hot-reload watcher\n│   ├── context/             ← context warming and pre-fetch\n│   │   ├── warmer.py            ← context warmer for active topics\n│   │   └── prefetch.py          ← speculative pre-fetch\n│   ├── conversation/        ← multi-turn conversation handling\n│   ├── diff/                ← diff generation for results\n│   ├── environment/         ← environment and repo discovery\n│   │   └── discovery.py        ← repo scanner: seeds registry.yaml (one-time/on-demand scan); self-mod agent is sole ongoing author (see Project Registry)\n│   ├── escalate/            ← escalate strand for task-profile intents\n│   │   ├── handler.py           ← escalate request handler\n│   │   ├── llm.py               ← LLM calls for bead formulation\n│   │   └── commands.py          ├── bead creation commands\n│   ├── feedback/            ← feedback processing and background analysis\n│   │   ├── processor.py         ← explicit feedback processor\n│   │   ├── signals.py           ← implicit feedback signal tracking\n│   │   └── background_analysis.py ← background analysis bead\n│   ├── fetch/               ← fetch strand (deterministic, per intent type)\n│   │   ├── commands.py          ← fetch command matrix, intent types, data structures\n│   │   └── orchestrator.py      ← FetchStrand: concurrent fetch execution, streaming, coverage tracking\n│   ├── intent/              ← intent router (LLM classification)\n│   │   └── router.py            ← intent segmentation and routing\n│   ├── memory/              ← memory store and extraction (supporting store for cross-session context; not yet surfaced by any component — Future Work)\n│   │   ├── store.py             ← memory persistence\n│   │   └── extraction.py        ← memory extraction from results\n│   ├── monitoring/          ← ambient monitoring\n│   │   └── ambient.py           ← ambient monitoring rules\n│   ├── realtime/            ← OpenAI Realtime API voice session\n│   │   ├── session.py           ← voice session handler\n│   │   ├── batching.py          ← result batching for narration\n│   │   ├── continuity.py        ← audio-to-canvas session continuity\n│   │   └── dispatch.py          ← tool-as-trigger dispatch\n│   ├── session/             ← session store (SQLite)\n│   │   └── store.py             ← session store operations\n│   ├── sse/                 ← SSE broadcasting\n│   │   ├── broadcaster.py       ← SSE connection registry\n│   │   └── events.py            ← SSE event types\n│   ├── stt/                 ← speech-to-text (whisper-stt browser fallback)\n│   │   └── fallback.py          ← STT fallback client behind /api/v1/stt\n│   ├── surface/             ← surface routing\n│   │   └── router.py            ← result surface routing logic\n│   ├── synthesize/          ← synthesize strand (LLM)\n│   │   └── strand.py            ← result synthesis\n│   ├── telegram/            ← Telegram fallback surface\n│   │   └── fallback.py          ← Telegram delivery\n│   ├── test/                ← test-harness endpoints (bypass Web Speech API for e2e tests)\n│   ├── topic/               ← topic model\n│   │   └── model.py             ← topic operations\n│   ├── watcher/             ← bead watcher daemon\n│   │   └── daemon.py            ← NEEDLE bead watcher\n│   ├── canvas/              ← web frontend (SSE consumer, card renderer)\n│   │   └── index.html           ← single-page canvas UI\n│   └── cli/                 ← adc CLI\n│       ├── main.py              ← CLI entry point\n│       ├── commands.py          ← CLI commands\n│       ├── config.py            ← CLI configuration\n│       └── sse.py               ← SSE streaming for CLI\n├── data/\n│   ├── session.db           ← session store (SQLite)\n│   └── components.db        ← component library (SQLite)\n└── docs/\n    ├── research/            ← architecture and design research\n    ├── plan/\n    │   └── plan.md          ← this file\n    └── notes/\n        ├── core-verification-evidence.md  ← phase verification evidence (cited by Implementation Phases)\n        └── naming.md\n```\n\n---\n\n## Deployment Model\n\naide-de-camp is a **live web application**, not a static site. The FastAPI server is the intelligence layer — it handles SSE connections, maintains session state, runs the router and synthesize strands, and serves the frontend HTML. There is no CDN path (no CF Pages).\n\n*Naming note: deployment stages are **Deploy Stage A** (bare-metal) and **Deploy Stage B** (k8s) — deliberately not \"Phase 0/1+\", which collided with the Implementation Phases numbering (deployment \"Phase 1+\" had nothing to do with implementation Phase 1). References elsewhere to the \"Phase 0 deployment\" (e.g., ADR-1) mean Deploy Stage A. Stage A is the hosting model through the screen-capture demo and public launch; Stage B is post-launch hardening.*\n\n### Why not static\n\n- SSE connections are long-lived; CDNs cannot proxy them\n- WebSocket for the Realtime API voice session requires a persistent backend\n- The session store (SQLite) and artifact store (prompts, registry) require a writable filesystem\n- Hot-reload depends on the running server reading updated files from disk on each invocation\n\n### Current Deployment: Deploy Stage A (bare-metal — Hetzner server directly)\n\n**Status: LIVE** ✅ — running server confirmed 2026-07-20 (ADR-1 audit)\n\nThe server runs as a process on the Hetzner server itself, not in k8s:\n- NEEDLE workers and the aide-de-camp server share the same filesystem\n- Self-modification agent writes directly to `prompts/` and `config/` — hot-reload works without any coordination\n- Exposed via Tailscale (the server is already on the mesh); no ingress config needed\n- SQLite DBs are local files; no PVC required\n\nRunning command: `uvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000`\n\n**Stage A process model — exactly one process, by design.** The command above is the complete process inventory. The bead watcher (which also hosts ambient monitoring and the async-path circuit breaker — see Bead Watcher) is **not** a second command an operator must remember: FastAPI's lifespan startup spawns the watcher loop as an asyncio background task inside this same uvicorn process. A separately documented daemon is exactly the step that gets skipped, and its absence is silent — closures never surface, SLAs never fire, monitoring never ticks, while every test still passes (the \"verified-in-tests, dead live\" pattern). Startup-spawning makes that failure impossible; liveness is still checked, not assumed: `GET /health` reports `watcher.alive` / `watcher.last_tick_at` (see Bead Watcher), and the pre-demo seeding runbook verifies it in item (3).\n\nNo container, no CI, no ArgoCD. Managed as a long-running process (see CLAUDE.md for restart commands).\n\n### Release flow (Deploy Stage A)\n\nVersion is in `pyproject.toml` only. No CI build — runs from source.\n\nRelease: `bump version in pyproject.toml` → `commit` → `git tag vX.Y.Z` → `push`.\n\n### Future: Deploy Stage B (containerized, ardenone-cluster)\n\n**Status: NOT BUILT** ❌\n\nSession persistence and multi-surface routing are implementation Phase 1 deliverables and run on Stage A (currently PARTIAL — see Implementation Phases); they are not the trigger for this stage. The Stage B trigger is operational: uptime independent of the Hetzner box, container isolation, and standard CI/ArgoCD management. Not needed for the demo or launch. When that point arrives, containerize and move to k8s:\n\n```\nDocker image: ronaldraygun/aide-de-camp\nDeployment: ardenone-cluster, namespace: aide-de-camp\nPVC: /data/ (SATA, ReadWriteOnce)\n  /data/session.db        ← session store\n  /data/components.db     ← component library\n  /data/prompts/          ← hot-reloadable prompt files\n  /data/config/           ← registry.yaml, monitoring.yaml, exceptions.yaml\n```\n\nThe artifact store (prompts, registry) moves from the repo's working directory to the PVC. The self-modification agent updates artifacts via the aide-de-camp API (`PATCH /artifacts/{name}`), which writes to the PVC path the server reads from. The PVC artifact directory is itself a git repository: the server commits every `PATCH /artifacts/{name}` write with the same machine-generated message convention, so the \"versioning is git\" safety model — one-instruction rollback, `adc restore-artifacts` — survives the move to Stage B unchanged.\n\n### Future: Traefik configuration for SSE and WebSocket (Deploy Stage B)\n\n**Status: NOT BUILT** ❌\n\nTraefik's default response buffering breaks SSE. The IngressRoute needs:\n\n```yaml\n# IngressRoute annotations\ntraefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: aide-de-camp-strip-prefix@kubernetescrd\n\n# Middleware: disable buffering, extend timeouts\napiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\nkind: Middleware\nmetadata:\n  name: aide-de-camp-streaming\nspec:\n  buffering:\n    maxResponseBodyBytes: 0   # disable buffering\n  headers:\n    customResponseHeaders:\n      X-Accel-Buffering: \"no\"\n```\n\nServersTransport timeout:\n\n```yaml\napiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1\nkind: ServersTransport\nmetadata:\n  name: aide-de-camp-longlived\nspec:\n  forwardingTimeouts:\n    responseHeaderTimeout: 0    # no timeout on SSE streams\n    dialTimeout: 5s\n```\n\nDUCK-E's existing WebSocket IngressRoute config is the reference implementation for this cluster.\n\n### SQLite concurrency\n\n**Status: COMPLETE** ✅\n\nMultiple writers (bead watcher, background analysis bead, session handler) require WAL mode to avoid lock contention:\n\n```python\n# On DB open\nconn.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\nconn.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n```\n\nWAL mode allows concurrent reads during writes and serializes multiple writers without blocking. For a personal single-user app, this is sufficient.\n\n### adc CLI connectivity\n\nThe `adc` CLI is a thin HTTP client. It talks to the running server over Tailscale:\n\n```bash\n# ~/.config/adc/config\nserver_url = \"http://localhost:8000\"   # Deploy Stage A (local)\n# Future: \"http://aide-de-camp.ardenone.com\" (Deploy Stage B)\n```\n\nNo local inference. The CLI sends requests to the FastAPI backend and streams the SSE response to the terminal.\n\nOne exception: the kill-switch fallbacks (`adc freeze`'s `data/FREEZE` sentinel, `adc restore-artifacts`' git revert) act on the server's local filesystem, not over HTTP — they only work when `adc` runs on the server host itself. SSH in first; see the Self-Modification Agent safety model's break-glass procedure.\n\n### Future: CI/CD (Deploy Stage B)\n\n**Status: NOT BUILT** ❌\n\nNew Argo WorkflowTemplate `aide-de-camp-build` on iad-ci:\n- Docker build → `ronaldraygun/aide-de-camp`\n- Update image tag in `jedarden/declarative-config` (k8s/ardenone-cluster/aide-de-camp/)\n- ArgoCD syncs automatically\n\nSame pattern as every other containerized service in the stack.\n\n---\n\n## Technology Stack\n\n| Layer | Choice | Reason |\n|-------|--------|--------|\n| Backend | FastAPI (Python) | Reuse DUCK-E scaffolding; SSE + WebSocket support; async-native |\n| STT | Web Speech API → whisper-stt fallback | Zero-latency browser-native; whisper-stt already deployed |\n| Voice session | OpenAI Realtime API (via DUCK-E session handler) | DUCK-E scaffolding already built on this |\n| LLM calls | ZAI proxy (`llm-proxy.ardenone.com`) | Pay-per-token, no subscription pressure on hot path |\n| Task workers | NEEDLE + Claude Code | Existing infrastructure, unchanged |\n| Session store | SQLite (WAL mode) | Lightweight, local, additive-only schema; single-user app |\n| Artifact store | PVC-mounted filesystem (Deploy Stage B) / local filesystem (Deploy Stage A) | Writable at runtime; hot-reload reads per-invocation |\n| Frontend | Vanilla JS + Web Components | No build pipeline; SSE consumer injects server-rendered component-card HTML. Client-side rendering is limited to the built-in card templates shipped with the served frontend (`src/canvas/`): the generic fallback card, the first-run welcome card, the pending/ack card with its progress states, the error/clarification cards (see Degraded-State UX), and the aged-pending treatment on overdue cards — every component-library card arrives server-rendered |\n| Deploy Stage A hosting | Hetzner server, local process | Simplest path; shared filesystem with NEEDLE workers |\n| Deploy Stage B hosting | ardenone-cluster, k8s Deployment | Behind existing Traefik + Tailscale ingress |\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Phases\n\n**Status vocabulary.** Every phase status below carries one of two verification tiers — never read \"COMPLETE\" without its tier:\n\n- **verified-in-tests** — deliverables pass the automated test harness / smoke tests (the `src/test/` endpoints that bypass the Web Speech API). Says nothing about behavior on the live server for a real user.\n- **verified-live** — observed working on the running server, with the date and how it was observed recorded on the status line.\n\nAs of 2026-07-22 no phase has reached verified-live end-to-end; the only live check on record (2026-07-20, ADR-1) disproved a Phase 1 deliverable.\n\n### Phase 0 — Minimal Viable Surface (~2 days)\n\n**Status: COMPLETE (verified-in-tests)** — not yet verified-live\n\n*Verification evidence:* see `docs/notes/core-verification-evidence.md` — smoke test results, 20+ runs with all tests passing (test harness, not live; date unrecorded — re-verify)\n\nValidates the core question: does routing + parallel dispatch reduce friction?\n\n- Single HTML page: textarea + mic button (Web Speech API)\n- `POST /dispatch` endpoint: router → N parallel synthesize calls → SSE stream\n- Results appear as basic cards as agents resolve\n- No persistence, no auth, no component library, no ambient push\n\nDeliverable: the core query loop working end-to-end.\n\n### Phase 1 — Session and Topics (~1 week)\n\n**Status: PARTIAL** ⚠️ — session store, topics, and bead watcher are verified-in-tests; the Telegram fallback deliverable is non-functional in every deployment to date (stub methods returning `False`, unreachable hardcoded bridge URL, architectural mismatch — see ADR-1, observed live 2026-07-20). Re-opened pending ADR-1 implementation.\n\n*Verification evidence:* see `docs/notes/core-verification-evidence.md` — session store, SSE, surface registration (test harness, not live; date unrecorded — re-verify). Those tests passed while the Telegram stubs shipped — this is exactly why the two-tier vocabulary above exists.\n\nResults persist; the canvas has memory.\n\n- Session store (SQLite, 7+ tables with topic_context_cache and feedback_signals)\n- Topic model: canvas shows one card per **(active topic, result_type)** pair, each updated in place — a status card and a brainstorm card on the same project topic coexist as distinct cards, grouped under the topic; a new result replaces only the card sharing both its topic and its result_type\n- Telegram surface fallback (reuse telegram-claude-bridge)\n- Bead watcher: closed NEEDLE beads push results to active surface\n- Workload summary on reconnect\n- Staleness indicators on cards\n\nDeliverable: sessions that survive browser refresh (verified-in-tests); Telegram fallback working (**not met** — see ADR-1).\n\n### Phase 2 — Self-Improvement Loop (~2 weeks)\n\n**Status: COMPLETE (verified-in-tests)** — not yet verified-live\n\n*Verification evidence:* see `docs/notes/core-verification-evidence.md` — component library, hot-reload manager (test harness, not live; _evaluate_auto_approve(self, request: EscalateRequest, exceptions_config: dict) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n178\t        \"\"\"\n179\t        Evaluate if an intent should be auto-approved based on exceptions.yaml.\n180\t\n181\t        Args:\n182\t            request: The escalate request\n183\t            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml config\n184\t\n185\t        Returns:\n186\t            (auto_approve: bool, reason: str | None)\n187\t        \"\"\"\n188\t        auto_approve_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"auto_approve\", {})\n189\t\n190\t        # Extract context for rule evaluation\n191\t        context = {\n192\t            \"environment\": request.metadata.get(\"environment\"),\n193\t            \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n194\t            \"branch\": request.metadata.get(\"branch\"),\n195\t            \"action\": request.metadata.get(\"action\"),\n196\t            \"risk_level\": request.metadata.get(\"risk_level\", \"normal\"),\n197\t        }\n198\t\n199\t        # Check read-only operations (always auto-approved)\n200\t        read_only_actions = auto_approve_rules.get(\"read_only\", [])\n201\t        action = context.get(\"action\")\n202\t        if action in read_only_actions:\n203\t            return True, f\"Read-only operation: {action}\"\n204\t\n205\t        # Check safe mutations with conditions\n206\t        safe_mutations = auto_approve_rules.get(\"safe_mutations\", [])\n207\t        for rule in safe_mutations:\n208\t            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n209\t            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n210\t\n211\t            # Simple condition evaluation (supports basic comparisons)\n212\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context) and action in actions:\n213\t                return True, f\"Safe mutation in {condition}: {action}\"\n214\t\n215\t        # Check manual approval rules (never auto-approve)\n216\t        manual_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"manual_approval\", [])\n217\t        for rule in manual_rules:\n218\t            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n219\t            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n220\t\n221\t            # Check if the action is in this rule's actions\n222\t            if action not in actions:\n223\t                continue\n224\t\n225\t            # Evaluate the condition\n226\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context):\n227\t                if rule.get(\"always_approve\") is False:\n228\t                    return False, f\"Manual approval required: {condition}\"\n229\t\n230\t        # Check approval workflow rules\n231\t        approval_config = exceptions_config.get(\"approval\", {})\n232\t        never_auto_approve = approval_config.get(\"never_auto_approve\", [])\n233\t        for condition_str in never_auto_approve:\n234\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition_str, context):\n235\t                return False, f\"Never auto-approve: {condition_str}\"\n236\t\n237\t        # Default: require manual approval for unknown actions\n238\t        return False, \"Unknown action - requires manual approval\"\n239\t\n240\t    def _evaluate_condition(self, condition: str, context: dict) -> bool:\n241\t        \"\"\"\n242\t        Evaluate a condition string against context.\n243\t\n244\t        Supports simple conditions like:\n245\t        - \"environment == 'staging'\"\n246\t        - \"branch == 'main' || branch == 'master'\"  (using || for OR)\n247\t        - \"action == 'kubectl_delete_namespace'\"\n248\t        - \"environment == 'staging' && action == 'restart'\"  (using && for AND)\n249\t\n250\t        Note: Supports both shell-style operators (||, &&) and Python-style (or, and).\n251\t\n252\t        Args:\n253\t            condition: The condition string\n254\t            context: Dict of available variables\n255\t\n256\t        Returns:\n257\t            True if condition evaluates to True, False otherwise\n258\t        \"\"\"\n259\t        try:\n260\t            # Translate shell-style operators to Python syntax\n261\t            # (People are more familiar with || and && from bash/js)\n262\t            python_condition = condition.replace(\" || \", \" or \").replace(\" && \", \" and \")\n263\t\n264\t            # Create a safe evaluation environment\n265\t            safe_globals = {\"__builtins__\": {}}\n266\t            safe_locals = {\n267\t                \"environment\": context.get(\"environment\"),\n268\t                \"project_slug\": context.get(\"project_slug\"),\n269\t                \"branch\": context.get(\"branch\"),\n270\t                \"action\": context.get(\"action\"),\n271\t                \"risk_level\": context.get(\"risk_level\"),\n272\t            }\n273\t\n274\t            # Evaluate condition\n275\t            result = eval(python_condition, safe_globals, safe_locals)\n276\t            return bool(result)\n277\t        except Exception as e:\n278\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to evaluate condition '{condition}': {e}\")\n279\t            return False\n280\t\n281\t    def _get_bead_type_from_targets(self, intent_type: str, exceptions_config: dict) -> str:\n282\t        \"\"\"\n283\t        Get the bead type from escalation_targets config.\n284\t\n285\t        Args:\n286\t            intent_type: The intent type from IntentRouter (e.g., 'action', 'task-profile', 'self-modification')\n287\t            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml request: EscalateRequest,\n441\t    ) -> str:\n442\t        \"\"\"\n443\t        Formulate a bead body using the LLM.\n444\t\n445\t        Takes the intent and context, produces a well-structured bead body.\n446\t        \"\"\"\n447\t        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n448\t        system_prompt = load_escalate_prompt()\n449\t\n450\t        # Build context for the LLM\n451\t        context_parts = [\n452\t            f\"User utterance: {request.utterance}\",\n453\t            f\"Intent type: {request.intent_type}\",\n454\t        ]\n455\t\n456\t        if request.project_slug:\n457\t            context_parts.append(f\"Project: {request.project_slug}\")\n458\t\n459\t        if request.topic_id:\n460\t            context_parts.append(f\"Topic ID: {request.topic_id}\")\n461\t\n462\t        # Add any additional context\n463\t        if request.context:\n464\t            context_parts.append(f\"\\nAdditional context:\")\n465\t            for key, value in request.context.items():\n466\t                context_parts.append(f\"  {key}: {value}\")\n467\t\n468\t        # Build the user message\n469\t        user_message = \"\\n\".join([\n470\t            \"Formulate a NEEDLE bead body for this intent:\",\n471\t            \"\",\n472\t            *context_parts,\n473\t            \"\",\n474\t            \"The bead body should be clear, actionable, and structured for Claude Code execution.\",\n475\t        ])\n476\t\n477\t        logger.info(f\"Formulating bead body for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n478\t\n479\t        # Make LLM call\n480\t        try:\n481\t            bead_body = await client.call_simple(\n482\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n483\t                user_message=user_message,\n484\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n485\t                max_tokens=4096,\n486\t                temperature=0.7,\n487\t            )\n488\t\n489\t            logger.info(f\"Formulated bead body for intent {request.intent_id}: {len(bead_body)} chars\")\n490\t            return bead_body\n491\t\n492\t        except Exception as e:\n493\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\")\n494\t            raise EscalateError(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\") from e\n495\t\n496\t    async def create_bead(\n497\t        self,\n498\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n499\t        bead_body: str,\n500\t    ) -> str:\n501\t        \"\"\"\n502\t        Create a bead using the bf CLI.\n503\t\n504\t        Returns the bead ID.\n505\t        \"\"\"\n506\t        # Generate bead title from intent\n507\t        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n508\t\n509\t        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n510\t        metadata = {\n511\t            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n512\t            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n513\t            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n514\t            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n515\t            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n516\t        }\n517\t\n518\t        if request.project_slug:\n519\t            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n520\t\n521\t        if request.topic_id:\n522\t            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n523\t\n524\t        # Build bf create command\n525\t        # Using bf CLI to create the bead\n526\t        # Note: bf uses --description for the main body content\n527\t        try:\n528\t            # Build command with description (escape bead body for shell)\n529\t            cmd = [\n530\t                \"bf\",\n531\t                \"create\",\n532\t                \"--title\", title,\n533\t                \"--type\", \"task\",  # task-profile intents create task beads\n534\t                \"--description\", bead_body,\n535\t            ]\n536\t\n537\t            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n538\t            # Labels are simple strings, we'll encode key=value for structured data\n539\t            for key, value in metadata.items():\n540\t                if value is not None:\n541\t                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n542\t\n543\t            # Add --project flag if project_slug is available\n544\t            # Per plan Beads-Workspace Scoping: all aide-de-camp-originated beads\n545\t            # are tagged with their target project as the --project field\n546\t            if request.project_slug:\n547\t                cmd.extend([\"--project\", request.project_slug])\n548\t\n549\t            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n550\t                *cmd,\n551\t                cwd=BEADS_WORKSPACE,  # Run from aide-de-camp workspace\n552\t                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n553\t                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n554\t            )\n555\t\n556\t            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n557\t\n558\t            if proc.returncode != 0:\n559\t                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n560\t                logger.error(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n561\t                raise BeadCreationError(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n562\t\n563\t            # Parse bead ID from output\n564\t            output = request.\"\"\"\n696\t        # Use utterance prefix as title\n697\t        utterance = request.utterance.strip()\n698\t        if len(utterance) > 60:\n699\t            title = utterance[:57] + output.\n711\t\n712\t        bf create outputs just the bead ID (e.g., \"abc-123\").\n713\t        May include prefix text like \"Created bead abc-123\" in some versions.\n714\t        \"\"\"\n715\t        import re\n716\t\n717\t        # Strip whitespace first\n718\t        output = output.strip()\n719\t\n720\t        if not output:\n721\t            logger.warning(f\"Empty bf create output\")\n722\t            return str(uuid.uuid4())\n723\t\n724\t        # Try to extract bead ID pattern: lowercase letters, dash, alphanumeric\n725\t        # Bead IDs typically include numbers like: abc-123, xyz-789, adc-2fs\n726\t        # We prioritize matches with digits over letter-only matches\n727\t        patterns = [\n728\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",  # Three components with digit: test-bead-456\n729\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",          # Two components with digit: abc-123\n730\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]+\",                  # Three components letter-only: test-bead-xyz\n731\t            r\"[a-z]{3,}-[a-z0-9]{2,}\",                   # Fallback: any 3+ char prefix, dash, 2+ char suffix\n732\t        ]\n733\t\n734\t        for pattern in patterns:\n735\t            matches = re.findall(pattern, output)\n736\t            if matches:\n737\t                # Return the longest match (prefer more specific patterns)\n738\t                bead_id = max(matches, key=len)\n739\t                logger.debug(f\"Extracted bead ID '{bead_id}' from output\")\n740\t                return bead_id\n741\t\n742\t        # Fallback: if output already looks like a bead ID, return as-is\n743\t        # Basic validation: should contain a dash and be reasonable length\n744\t        if \"-\" in output and len(output) >= 3:\n745\t            logger.warning(f\"Using output as bead ID despite non-standard format: {output}\")\n746\t            return output\n747\t\n748\t        # Last resort: return the whole output (bf might have changed format)\n749\t        logger.warning(f\"Unexpected bf create output format: {output}\")\n750\t        return output\n751\t\n752\t    async def _validate_and_prepare_approval(\n753\t        self,\n754\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n755\t        bead_body: str,\n756\t        bead_type: str,\n757\t    ) -> tuple[ValidationResult, str | None]:\n758\t        \"\"\"\n759\t        Validate bead body and prepare for approval if needed.\n760\t\n761\t        Args:\n762\t            request: The escalate request\n763\t            bead_body: The formulated bead body\n764\t            bead_type: The bead type\n765\t\n766\t        Returns:\n767\t            (validation_result, reformulated_bead_body_or_None)\n768\t\n769\t        Raises:\n770\t            BeadApprovalRequired: When bead requires approval (carries approval card)\n771\t            ValidationRetryExhaustedError: When validation fails after re-formulation\n772\t        \"\"\"\n773\t        validator = get_validator()\n774\t\n775\t        # Initial validation\n776\t        result = validator.validate_bead_body(bead_body, bead_type)\n777\t\n778\t        if None\n781\t\n782\t        if result.requires_approval:\n783\t            logger.info(f\"Validation passed but approval required for intent {request.intent_id}: {result.approval_requirement.reason}\")\n784\t            # Build approval card\n785\t            approval_card = self._build_approval_card(\n786\t                request=request,\n787\t bead_body, bead_type)\n792\t\n793\t        # Validation failed - check re-formulation count before attempting\n794\t        store = await self._get_store()\n795\t        reformulation_count = await store.get_reformulation_count(request.session_id)\n796\t        MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS = 3  # Maximum 3 attempts per session\n797\t\n798\t        if reformulation_count >= MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS:\n799\t            logger.error(\n800\t                f\"Session {request.session_id} has exceeded re-formulation attempt limit \"\n801\t                f\"({reformulation_count}/{MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS})\"\n802\t            )\n803\t            raise ValidationRetryExhaustedError(\n804\t                result.violations,\n805\t                f\"Re-formulation attempt limit exceeded ({MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS} attempts per session). \"\n806\t                \"Please clarify your request or provide more specific details.\",\n807\t            )\n808\t\n809\t        # Increment re-formulation count\n810\t        new_count = await store.increment_reformulation_count(request.session_id)\n811\t        logger.info(f\"Attempt re-formulation for intent {request.intent_id} (session attempt {new_count}/{MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS})\")\n812\t        logger.warning(f\"Validation failed for intent {request.intent_id}, attempting re-formulation\")\n813\t        logger.info(f\"Reformulation hint: {result.reformulation_hint}\")\n814\t\n815\t        try:\n816\t            # Re-formulate with failure reason\n817\t            reformulated_body = await self._reformulate_with_failure_reason(\n818\t                request=request,\n819\t                original_body=bead_body,\n820\t                validation_result=result,\n821\t            )\n822\t\n823\t            # Validate the reformulated body\n824\t            new_result = validator.validate_bead_body(reformulated_body, bead_type)\n825\t\n826\t            if not new_result.is_valid:\n827\t                # Re-formulation still failed\n828\t                logger.error(f\"Re-formulation still failed for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n829\t                raise ValidationRetryExhaustedError(\n830\t                    new_result.violations,\n831\t                    f\"Validation failed after re-formulation: {len(new_result.violations)} violations remain\",\n832\t                )\n833\t\n834\t            if new_result.requires_approval:\n835\t                logger.info(f\"Re-formulated body requires approval\")\n836\t                approval_card = self._build_approval_card(\n837\t                    request=request,\n838\t                    bead_body=reformulated_body,\n839\t                    bead_type=bead_type,\n840\t                    validation_result=new_result,\n841\t                )\n842\t                raise BeadApprovalRequired(approval_card, reformulated_body, bead_type)\n843\t\n844\t            logger.info(f\"Re-formulated body passed validation\")\n845\t            return new_result, reformulated_body\n846\t\n847\t        except Exception as e:\n848\t            logger.error(f\"Re-formulation failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n849\t            raise ValidationRetryExhaustedError(\n850\t                result.violations,\n851\t                f\"Re-formulation failed: {str(e)}\",\n852\t            )\n853\t\n854\t    async def _reformulate_with_failure_reason(\n855\t        self,\n856\t        request: EscalateRequest,\n857\t        original_body: str,\n858\t        validation_result: ValidationResult,\n859\t    ) -> str:\n860\t        \"\"\"\n861\t        Re-formulate bead body with validation failure reason.\n862\t\n863\t        Args:\n864\t            request: The escalate request\n865\t            original_body: The original bead body\n866\t            validation_result: The validation result with violations\n867\t\n868\t        Returns:\n869\t            Reformulated bead body\n870\t        \"\"\"\n871\t        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n872\t        system_prompt = load_escalate_prompt()\n873\t\n874\t },\n1043\t        }\n1044\t\n1045\t    async def escalate_intent(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n1046\t        \"\"\"\n1047\t        Escalate an intent to a bead.\n1048\t\n1049\t        Full flow:\n1050\t        1. Load exceptions.yaml to determine bead type from escalation_targets\n1051\t        2. Evaluate auto-approve rules from exceptions.yaml\n1052\t        3. If auto-approved: execute directly and return result\n1053\t        4. Otherwise: formulate bead body via LLM, validate, create bead (or approval card), return result\n1054\t\n1055\t        Args:\n1056\t            request: The escalate request\n1057\t\n1058\t        Returns:\n1059\t            EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card (if bead created)\n1060\t            or approval card (if approval required)\n1061\t            or execution result (if auto-approved)\n1062\t        \"\"\"\n1063\t        logger.info(f\"Escalating intent {request.intent_id}\")\n1064\t\n1065\t        try:\n1066\t            # Step 1: Load exceptions.yaml\n1067\t            reload_mgr = self._get_reload_manager()\n1068\t            exceptions_config = reload_mgr.get_config(\"exceptions\")\n1069\t\n1070\t            # Step 2: Evaluate auto-approve rules\n1071\t            auto_approve, reason = self._evaluate_auto_approve(request, exceptions_config)\n1072\t\n1073\t            if auto_approve:\n1074\t                logger.info(f\"Auto-approved intent {request.intent_id}: {reason}\")\n1075\t\n1076\t                # Execute directly without bead creation\n1077\t                execution_result = await self._execute_auto_approved(request)\n1078\t\n1079\t                # Update intent in store\n1080\t                store = await self._get_store()\n1081\t                await store.update_intent_status(\n1082\t                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n1083\t                    status=\"resolved\",\n1084\t                )\n1085\t\n1086\t                # Return result with empty pending_card (auto-approved, no bead created)\n1087\t                return EscalateResult(\n1088\t                    bead_id=\"\",  # No bead created\n1089\t                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n1090\t                    pending_card=execution_result,  # Return execution result directly\n1091\t                    status=\"completed\",\n1092\t                )\n1093\t\n1094\t            # Step 3: Determine bead type from escalation_targets\n1095\t            bead_type = self._get_bead_type_from_targets(request.intent_type, exceptions_config)\n1096\t            logger.info(f\"Creating bead type '{bead_type}' for intent {request.intent_id} (not auto-approved: {reason})\")\n1097\t\n1098\t            # Step 4: Formulate bead body\n1099\t            bead_body = await self.formulate_bead_body(request)\n1100\t\n1101\t            # Step 4.5: Validate and prepare for approval (NEW)\n1102\t            validation_result, reformulated_body = await Return pending-card spec (if bead created) or execution result\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None):\n        self.store = store\n        self._zai_client = None\n
