self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _evaluate_auto_approve(self, request: EscalateRequest, exceptions_config: dict) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evaluate if an intent should be auto-approved based on exceptions.yaml.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml config\n\n        Returns:\n            (auto_approve: bool, reason: str | None)\n        \"\"\"\n        auto_approve_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"auto_approve\", {})\n\n        # Extract context for rule evaluation\n        context = {\n            \"environment\": request.metadata.get(\"environment\"),\n            \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n            \"branch\": request.metadata.get(\"branch\"),\n            \"action\": request.metadata.get(\"action\"),\n            \"risk_level\": request.metadata.get(\"risk_level\", \"normal\"),\n        }\n\n        # Check read-only operations (always auto-approved)\n        read_only_actions = auto_approve_rules.get(\"read_only\", [])\n        action = context.get(\"action\")\n        if action in read_only_actions:\n            return True, f\"Read-only operation: {action}\"\n\n        # Check safe mutations with conditions\n        safe_mutations = auto_approve_rules.get(\"safe_mutations\", [])\n        for rule in safe_mutations:\n            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n\n            # Simple condition evaluation (supports basic comparisons)\n            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context) and action in actions:\n                return True, f\"Safe mutation in {condition}: {action}\"\n\n        # Check manual approval rules (never auto-approve)\n        manual_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"manual_approval\", [])\n        for rule in manual_rules:\n            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n\n            # Check if the action is in this rule's actions\n            if action not in actions:\n                continue\n\n            # Evaluate the condition\n            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context):\n                if rule.get(\"always_approve\") is False:\n                    return False, f\"Manual approval required: {condition}\"\n\n        # Check approval workflow rules\n        approval_config = exceptions_config.get(\"approval\", {})\n        never_auto_approve = approval_config.get(\"never_auto_approve\", [])\n        for condition_str in never_auto_approve:\n            if self._evaluate_condition(condition_str, context):\n                return False, f\"Never auto-approve: {condition_str}\"\n\n        # Default: require manual approval for unknown actions\n        return False, \"Unknown action - requires manual approval\"\n\n    def _evaluate_condition(self, condition: str, context: dict) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evaluate a condition string against context.\n\n        Supports simple conditions like:\n        - \"environment == 'staging'\"\n        - \"branch == 'main' || branch == 'master'\"  (using || for OR)\n        - \"action == 'kubectl_delete_namespace'\"\n        - \"environment == 'staging' && action == 'restart'\"  (using && for AND)\n\n        Note: Supports both shell-style operators (||, &&) and Python-style (or, and).\n\n        Args:\n            condition: The condition string\n            context: Dict of available variables\n\n        Returns:\n            True if condition evaluates to True, False otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Translate shell-style operators to Python syntax\n            # (People are more familiar with || and && from bash/js)\n            python_condition = condition.replace(\" || \", \" or \").replace(\" && \", \" and \")\n\n            # Create a safe evaluation environment\n            safe_globals = {\"__builtins__\": {}}\n            safe_locals = {\n                \"environment\": context.get(\"environment\"),\n                \"project_slug\": context.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                \"branch\": context.get(\"branch\"),\n                \"action\": context.get(\"action\"),\n                \"risk_level\": context.get(\"risk_level\"),\n            }\n\n            # Evaluate condition\n            result = eval(python_condition, safe_globals, safe_locals)\n            return bool(result)\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to evaluate condition '{condition}': {e}\")\n            return False\n\n    def _get_bead_type_from_targets(self, intent_type: str, exceptions_config: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get the bead type from escalation_targets config.\n\n        Args:\n            intent_type: The intent type from IntentRouter (e.g., 'action', 'task-profile', 'self-modification')\n            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml config\n\n        Returns:\n            The bead type to create (default: 'task')\n        \"\"\"\n        # Map IntentRouter types to escalation_targets keys\n        intent_type_mapping = {\n            \"action\": \"action\",\n            \"task-profile\": \"action\",  # Task-profile intents create action-type beads\n            \"self-modification\": \"self_modification\",\n            \"monitoring-config\": \"monitoring_config\",\n        }\n\n        # Map the intent type to escalation_targets key\n        escalation_key = intent_type_mapping.get(intent_type, intent_type)\n\n        escalation_targets = exceptions_config.get(\"escalation_targets\", {})\n        target_config = escalation_targets.get(escalation_key, {})\n        return target_config.get(\"bead_type\", \"task\")\n\n    async def _execute_auto_approved(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute an auto-approved action directly.\n\n        Executes kubectl and git commands that are auto-approved based on\n        exceptions.yaml rules, without creating beads.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            Execution result dict\n        \"\"\"\n        action = request.metadata.get(\"action\", \"\")\n        logger.info(f\"Executing auto-approved action '{action}' for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        try:\n            # Route to appropriate executor based on action type\n            if action == \"kubectl_delete_pod\":\n                return await self._execute_delete_pod(request)\n            elif action.startswith(\"kubectl_\"):\n                return await self._execute_kubectl_command(request)\n            elif action.startswith(\"git_\"):\n                return await self._execute_git_command(request)\n            else:\n                # Unknown action - return placeholder for now\n                logger.warning(f\"Unknown auto-approved action: {action}\")\n                return {\n                    \"status\": \"pending\",\n                    \"summary\": f\"Auto-approved action '{action}' not yet implemented\",\n                    \"data\": {\n                        \"action\": action,\n                        \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n                    },\n                    \"urgency\": \"low\",\n                }\n\n        except CommandExecutionError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Command execution failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"status\": \"failed\",\n                \"summary\": f\"Command execution failed: {str(e)}\",\n                \"data\": {\n                    \"action\": action,\n                    \"error\": str(e),\n                },\n                \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n            }\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Unexpected error executing auto-approved action: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"status\": \"failed\",\n                \"summary\": f\"Unexpected error: {str(e)}\",\n                \"data\": {\n                    \"action\": action,\n                    \"error\": str(e),\n                },\n                \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n            }\n\n    async def _execute_delete_pod(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute kubectl delete pod command.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            Execution result dict\n        \"\"\"\n        executor = get_kubectl_executor()\n\n        # Parse utterance to extract pod name and namespace\n        params = executor.parse_delete_pod_utterance(\n            utterance=request.utterance,\n            project_slug=request.project_slug,\n        )\n\n        # Execute the delete command\n        result = await executor.execute_delete_pod(\n            pod_name=params[\"pod_name\"],\n            namespace=params[\"namespace\"],\n            project_slug=request.project_slug,\n        )\n\n        return result\n\n    async def _execute_kubectl_command(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute other kubectl commands (placeholder for future).\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            Execution result dict\n        \"\"\"\n        # TODO: Implement other kubectl commands\n        action = request.metadata.get(\"action\", \"\")\n        return {\n            \"status\": \"pending\",\n            \"summary\": f\"Kubectl command '{action}' not yet implemented\",\n            \"data\": {\n                \"action\": action,\n                \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n            },\n            \"urgency\": \"low\",\n        }\n\n    async def _execute_git_command(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute git commands (placeholder for future).\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            Execution result dict\n        \"\"\"\n        # TODO: Implement git commands\n        action = request.metadata.get(\"action\", \"\")\n        return {\n            \"status\": \"pending\",\n            \"summary\": f\"Git command '{action}' not yet implemented\",\n            \"data\": {\n                \"action\": action,\n                \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n            },\n            \"urgency\": \"low\",\n        }\n\n    async def formulate_bead_body(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Formulate a bead body using the LLM.\n\n        Takes the intent and context, produces a well-structured bead body.\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n        system_prompt = load_escalate_prompt()\n\n        # Build context for the LLM\n        context_parts = [\n            f\"User utterance: {request.utterance}\",\n            f\"Intent type: {request.intent_type}\",\n        ]\n\n        if request.project_slug:\n            context_parts.append(f\"Project: {request.project_slug}\")\n\n        if request.topic_id:\n            context_parts.append(f\"Topic ID: {request.topic_id}\")\n\n        # Add any additional context\n        if request.context:\n            context_parts.append(f\"\\nAdditional context:\")\n            for key, value in request.context.items():\n                context_parts.append(f\"  {key}: {value}\")\n\n        # Build the user message\n        user_message = \"\\n\".join([\n            \"Formulate a NEEDLE bead body for this intent:\",\n            \"\",\n            *context_parts,\n            \"\",\n            \"The bead body should be clear, actionable, and structured for Claude Code execution.\",\n        ])\n\n        logger.info(f\"Formulating bead body for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        # Make LLM call\n        try:\n            bead_body = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=4096,\n                temperature=0.7,\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Formulated bead body for intent {request.intent_id}: {len(bead_body)} chars\")\n            return bead_body\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\")\n            raise EscalateError(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def create_bead(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_body: str,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a bead using the bf CLI.\n\n        Returns the bead ID.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate bead title from intent\n        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n\n        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n        metadata = {\n            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n        }\n\n        if request.project_slug:\n            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n\n        if request.topic_id:\n            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n\n        # Build bf create command\n        # Using bf CLI to create the bead\n        # Note: bf uses --description for the main body content\n        try:\n            # Build command with description (escape bead body for shell)\n            cmd = [\n                \"bf\",\n                \"create\",\n                \"--title\", title,\n                \"--type\", \"task\",  # task-profile intents create task beads\n                \"--description\", bead_body,\n            ]\n\n            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n            # Labels are simple strings, we'll encode key=value for structured data\n            for key, value in metadata.items():\n                if value is not None:\n                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n\n            # Add --project flag if project_slug is available\n            # Per plan Beads-Workspace Scoping: all aide-de-camp-originated beads\n            # are tagged with their target project as the --project field\n            if request.project_slug:\n                cmd.extend([\"--project\", request.project_slug])\n\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *cmd,\n                cwd=BEADS_WORKSPACE,  # Run from aide-de-camp workspace\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n                logger.error(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n                raise BeadCreationError(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n\n            # Parse bead ID from output\n            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n            return bead_id\n\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            logger.error(\"bf CLI not found\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(\"bf CLI not found\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def _create_bead_with_type(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_body: str,\n        bead_type: str,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a bead with a specific type using the bf CLI.\n\n        Returns the bead ID.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate bead title from intent\n        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n\n        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n        metadata = {\n            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n        }\n\n        if request.project_slug:\n            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n\n        if request.topic_id:\n            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n\n        # Build bf create command\n        try:\n            cmd = [\n                \"bf\",\n                \"create\",\n                \"--title\", title,\n                \"--type\", bead_type,\n                \"--description\", bead_body,\n            ]\n\n            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n            for key, value in metadata.items():\n                if value is not None:\n                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n\n            # Add --project flag if project_slug is available\n            # Per plan Beads-Workspace Scoping: all aide-de-camp-originated beads\n            # are tagged with their target project as the --project field\n            if request.project_slug:\n                cmd.extend([\"--project\", request.project_slug])\n\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *cmd,\n                cwd=BEADS_WORKSPACE,  # Run from aide-de-camp workspace\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n                logger.error(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n                raise BeadCreationError(f\"bf create failed: {error_msg}\")\n\n            # Parse bead ID from output\n            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} (type: {bead_type}) for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n            return bead_id\n\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            logger.error(\"bf CLI not found\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(\"bf CLI not found\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def _create_bead_watch(\n        self,\n        bead_ref: str,\n        project_slug: str | None,\n        intent_type: str,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Create bead_watch row for circuit breaker tracking.\n\n        Plan §10 The Async Path: watcher tracks open beads for refusals/SLA.\n        SLA defaults by intent_type, with per-project sla_hours override.\n\n        Args:\n            bead_ref: The bead ID to watch\n            project_slug: Project slug for SLA override lookup\n            intent_type: Intent type for default SLA lookup\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..registry import get_project\n\n        sla_hours = None\n\n        # Check for per-project SLA override\n        if project_slug:\n            project = get_project(project_slug)\n            if project:\n                sla_hours = project.get(\"sla_hours\")\n                if sla_hours is not None:\n                    logger.info(\n                        f\"Using per-project SLA override for {project_slug}: \"\n                        f\"{sla_hours} hours\"\n                    )\n\n        store = await self._get_store()\n        await store.create_bead_watch(\n            bead_ref=bead_ref,\n            sla_hours=sla_hours,\n            intent_type=intent_type,\n        )\n        logger.debug(f\"Created bead_watch row for {bead_ref}\")\n\n    def _generate_bead_title(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Generate a bead title from the request.\"\"\"\n        # Use utterance prefix as title\n        utterance = request.utterance.strip()\n        if len(utterance) > 60:\n            title = utterance[:57] + \"...\"\n        else:\n            title = utterance\n\n        # Add project prefix if available\n        if request.project_slug:\n            title = f\"[{request.project_slug}] {title}\"\n\n        return title\n\n    def _extract_bead_id(self, output: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Extract bead ID from bf create output.\n\n        bf create outputs just the bead ID (e.g., \"abc-123\").\n        May include prefix text like \"Created bead abc-123\" in some versions.\n        \"\"\"\n        import re\n\n        # Strip whitespace first\n        output = output.strip()\n\n        if not output:\n            logger.warning(f\"Empty bf create output\")\n            return str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n        # Try to extract bead ID pattern: lowercase letters, dash, alphanumeric\n        # Bead IDs typically include numbers like: abc-123, xyz-789, adc-2fs\n        # We prioritize matches with digits over letter-only matches\n        patterns = [\n            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",  # Three components with digit: test-bead-456\n            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",          # Two components with digit: abc-123\n            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]+\",                  # Three components letter-only: test-bead-xyz\n            r\"[a-z]{3,}-[a-z0-9]{2,}\",                   # Fallback: any 3+ char prefix, dash, 2+ char suffix\n        ]\n\n        for pattern in patterns:\n            matches = re.findall(pattern, output)\n            if matches:\n                # Return the longest match (prefer more specific patterns)\n                bead_id = max(matches, key=len)\n                logger.debug(f\"Extracted bead ID '{bead_id}' from output\")\n                return bead_id\n\n        # Fallback: if output already looks like a bead ID, return as-is\n        # Basic validation: should contain a dash and be reasonable length\n        if \"-\" in output and len(output) >= 3:\n            logger.warning(f\"Using output as bead ID despite non-standard format: {output}\")\n            return output\n\n        # Last resort: return the whole output (bf might have changed format)\n        logger.warning(f\"Unexpected bf create output format: {output}\")\n        return output\n\n    async def _validate_and_prepare_approval(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_body: str,\n        bead_type: str,\n    ) -> tuple[ValidationResult, str | None]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Validate bead body and prepare for approval if needed.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n            bead_body: The formulated bead body\n            bead_type: The bead type\n\n        Returns:\n            (validation_result, reformulated_bead_body_or_None)\n\n        Raises:\n            BeadApprovalRequired: When bead requires approval (carries approval card)\n            ValidationRetryExhaustedError: When validation fails after re-formulation\n        \"\"\"\n        validator = get_validator()\n\n        # Initial validation\n        result = validator.validate_bead_body(bead_body, bead_type)\n\n        if result.is_valid and not result.requires_approval:\n            logger.info(f\"Validation passed for intent {request.intent_id} (no approval required)\")\n            return result, None\n\n        if result.requires_approval:\n            logger.info(f\"Validation passed but approval required for intent {request.intent_id}: {result.approval_requirement.reason}\")\n            # Build approval card\n            approval_card = self._build_approval_card(\n                request=request,\n                bead_body=bead_body,\n                bead_type=bead_type,\n                validation_result=result,\n            )\n            raise BeadApprovalRequired(approval_card, bead_body, bead_type)\n\n        # Validation failed - check re-formulation count before attempting\n        store = await self._get_store()\n        reformulation_count = await store.get_reformulation_count(request.session_id)\n        MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS = 3  # Maximum 3 attempts per session\n\n        if reformulation_count >= MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS:\n            logger.error(\n                f\"Session {request.session_id} has exceeded re-formulation attempt limit \"\n                f\"({reformulation_count}/{MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS})\"\n            )\n            raise ValidationRetryExhaustedError(\n                result.violations,\n                f\"Re-formulation attempt limit exceeded ({MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS} attempts per session). \"\n                \"Please clarify your request or provide more specific details.\",\n            )\n\n        # Increment re-formulation count\n        new_count = await store.increment_reformulation_count(request.session_id)\n        logger.info(f\"Attempt re-formulation for intent {request.intent_id} (session attempt {new_count}/{MAX_REFORMULATION_ATTEMPTS})\")\n        logger.warning(f\"Validation failed for intent {request.intent_id}, attempting re-formulation\")\n        logger.info(f\"Reformulation hint: {result.reformulation_hint}\")\n\n        try:\n            # Re-formulate with failure reason\n            reformulated_body = await self._reformulate_with_failure_reason(\n                request=request,\n                original_body=bead_body,\n                validation_result=result,\n            )\n\n            # Validate the reformulated body\n            new_result = validator.validate_bead_body(reformulated_body, bead_type)\n\n            if not new_result.is_valid:\n                # Re-formulation still failed\n                logger.error(f\"Re-formulation still failed for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n                raise ValidationRetryExhaustedError(\n                    new_result.violations,\n                    f\"Validation failed after re-formulation: {len(new_result.violations)} violations remain\",\n                )\n\n            if new_result.requires_approval:\n                logger.info(f\"Re-formulated body requires approval\")\n                approval_card = self._build_approval_card(\n                    request=request,\n                    bead_body=reformulated_body,\n                    bead_type=bead_type,\n                    validation_result=new_result,\n                )\n                raise BeadApprovalRequired(approval_card, reformulated_body, bead_type)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Re-formulated body passed validation\")\n            return new_result, reformulated_body\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Re-formulation failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            raise ValidationRetryExhaustedError(\n                result.violations,\n                f\"Re-formulation failed: {str(e)}\",\n            )\n\n    async def _reformulate_with_failure_reason(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        original_body: str,\n        validation_result: ValidationResult,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Re-formulate bead body with validation failure reason.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n            original_body: The original bead body\n            validation_result: The validation result with violations\n\n        Returns:\n            Reformulated bead body\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n        system_prompt = load_escalate_prompt()\n\n        # Build failure message\n        failure_message = \"The previous bead body failed validation:\\n\\n\"\n        for violation in validation_result.violations:\n            failure_message += f\"- {violation.message}\\n\"\n        if validation_result.reformulation_hint:\n            failure_message += f\"\\nHint: {validation_result.reformulation_hint}\"\n\n        # Build user message\n        user_message = f\"\"\"Original intent: {request.utterance}\n\n{failure_message}\n\nPlease re-formulate the bead body to address these validation errors.\nOutput ONLY the revised bead body as markdown.\n\"\"\"\n\n        logger.info(f\"Requesting re-formulation for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        try:\n            reformulated = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=4096,\n                temperature=0.7,\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Re-formulated bead body for intent {request.intent_id}: {len(reformulated)} chars\")\n            return reformulated\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to re-formulate bead body: {e}\")\n            raise\n\n    def _build_approval_card(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_body: str,\n        bead_type: str,\n        validation_result: ValidationResult,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Build an approval card for the surface.\n\n        The approval card shows the bead body and asks for user approval.\n        \"\"\"\n        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n\n        return {\n            \"type\": \"approval\",\n            \"id\": f\"approval-{request.intent_id}\",\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"title\": title,\n            \"summary\": f\"Review before creating: {request.utterance[:100]}\",\n            \"status\": \"awaiting_approval\",\n            \"urgency\": request.metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n            \"bead_body\": bead_body,\n            \"bead_type\": bead_type,\n            \"validation_result\": validation_result.to_dict(),\n            \"metadata\": {\n                \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n                \"topic_id\": request.topic_id,\n                \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n                \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n            },\n        }\n\n    def _build_approval_narration(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_type: str,\n        validation_result: dict,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Build an approval narration for voice mode.\n\n        Creates a spoken message explaining why approval is needed.\n        \"\"\"\n        bead_type_friendly = bead_type.replace(\"_\", \" \")\n\n        # Start with the core message\n        narration_parts = [\n            f\"I need your approval before I can create this {bead_type_friendly} bead.\",\n        ]\n\n        # Add the reason if available\n        if validation_result and validation_result.get(\"approval_requirement\"):\n            approval_req = validation_result[\"approval_requirement\"]\n            reason = approval_req.get(\"reason\", \"\")\n            if reason:\n                narration_parts.append(f\"Reason: {reason}\")\n\n        # Add the request summary\n        utterance_preview = request.utterance[:100]\n        if len(request.utterance) > 100:\n            utterance_preview += \"...\"\n        narration_parts.append(f\"Request: {utterance_preview}\")\n\n        # Add instructions\n        narration_parts.append(\"Please approve or reject this request on the canvas.\")\n\n        return \" \".join(narration_parts)\n\n    def build_pending_card(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_id: str,\n        bead_type: str = \"task\",\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Build a pending-card spec for the surface.\n\n        The pending card shows the user that work is in progress\n        and will be delivered when the bead closes.\n        \"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"type\": \"pending\",\n            \"id\": f\"pending-{bead_id}\",\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n            \"title\": self._generate_bead_title(request),\n            \"summary\": f\"Working on: {request.utterance[:100]}\",\n            \"status\": \"pending\",\n            \"urgency\": request.metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n            \"metadata\": {\n                \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n                \"topic_id\": request.topic_id,\n                \"bead_type\": bead_type,\n            },\n        }\n\n    def build_clarification_card(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        original_bead_body: str,\n        violations: list,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Build a clarification card for the surface.\n\n        The clarification card shows the user that the intent needs\n        clarification due to validation failures after re-formulation.\n        \"\"\"\n        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n\n        # Format violations for display\n        violation_messages = []\n        for v in violations:\n            violation_messages.append(f\"- {v.message}\")\n\n        return {\n            \"type\": \"clarification\",\n            \"id\": f\"clarification-{request.intent_id}\",\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"title\": title,\n            \"summary\": f\"Needs clarification: {request.utterance[:100]}\",\n            \"status\": \"needs_clarification\",\n            \"urgency\": request.metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n            \"original_utterance\": request.utterance,\n            \"original_bead_body\": original_bead_body,\n            \"violations\": violation_messages,\n            \"metadata\": {\n                \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n                \"topic_id\": request.topic_id,\n                \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n            },\n        }\n\n    async def escalate_intent(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Escalate an intent to a bead.\n\n        Full flow:\n        1. Load exceptions.yaml to determine bead type from escalation_targets\n        2. Evaluate auto-approve rules from exceptions.yaml\n        3. If auto-approved: execute directly and return result\n        4. Otherwise: formulate bead body via LLM, validate, create bead (or approval card), return result\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card (if bead created)\n            or approval card (if approval required)\n            or execution result (if auto-approved)\n        \"\"\"\n        logger.info(f\"Escalating intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Load exceptions.yaml\n            reload_mgr = self._get_reload_manager()\n            exceptions_config = reload_mgr.get_config(\"exceptions\")\n\n            # Step 2: Evaluate auto-approve rules\n            auto_approve, reason = self._evaluate_auto_approve(request, exceptions_config)\n\n            if auto_approve:\n                logger.info(f\"Auto-approved intent {request.intent_id}: {reason}\")\n\n                # Execute directly without bead creation\n                execution_result = await self._execute_auto_approved(request)\n\n                # Update intent in store\n                store = await self._get_store()\n                await store.update_intent_status(\n                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                    status=\"resolved\",\n                )\n\n                # Return result with empty pending_card (auto-approved, no bead created)\n                return EscalateResult(\n                    bead_id=\"\",  # No bead created\n                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                    pending_card=execution_result,  # Return execution result directly\n                    status=\"completed\",\n                )\n\n            # Step 3: Determine bead type from escalation_targets\n            bead_type = self._get_bead_type_from_targets(request.intent_type, exceptions_config)\n            logger.info(f\"Creating bead type '{bead_type}' for intent {request.intent_id} (not auto-approved: {reason})\")\n\n            # Step 4: Formulate bead body\n            bead_body = await self.formulate_bead_body(request)\n\n            # Step 4.5: Validate and prepare for approval (NEW)\n            validation_result, reformulated_body = await self._validate_and_prepare_approval(\n                request=request,\n                bead_body=bead_body,\n                bead_type=bead_type,\n            )\n\n            # Use reformulated body if provided, otherwise original\n            final_body = reformulated_body or bead_body\n\n            # Step 5: Create bead with determined type\n            bead_id = await self._create_bead_with_type(request, final_body, bead_type)\n\n            # Step 5.5: Create bead_watch row for circuit breaker tracking\n            # (plan §10 The Async Path: watcher tracks open beads for refusals/SLA)\n            await self._create_bead_watch(\n                bead_ref=bead_id,\n                project_slug=request.project_slug,\n                intent_type=request.intent_type,\n            )\n\n            # Reset re-formulation count on successful bead creation\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.reset_reformulation_count(request.session_id)\n            logger.info(f\"Reset reformulation_count for session {request.session_id} after successful bead creation\")\n\n            # Step 6: Build pending card\n            pending_card = self.build_pending_card(request, bead_id, bead_type)\n\n            # Update intent in store with bead reference\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                status=\"dispatched\",\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Escalated intent {request.intent_id} to bead {bead_id}\")\n\n            return EscalateResult(\n                bead_id=bead_id,\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                pending_card=pending_card,\n                status=\"created\",\n            )\n\n        except BeadApprovalRequired as e:\n            # Approval required - store pending approval and broadcast approval event\n            logger.info(f\"Approval required for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n            # Update intent status\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                status=\"awaiting_approval\",\n            )\n\n            # Store pending approval in database\n            approval_id = await store.create_pending_approval(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                session_id=request.session_id,\n                bead_body=e.bead_body,\n                bead_type=e.bead_type,\n                validation_result=e.approval_card.get(\"validation_result\", {}),\n                utterance=request.utterance,\n                project_slug=request.project_slug,\n                topic_id=request.topic_id,\n            )\n            logger.info(f\"Stored pending approval {approval_id} for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n            # Add approval_id to approval card for reference\n            e.approval_card[\"approval_id\"] = approval_id\n\n            # Build approval narration for voice mode\n            approval_narration = self._build_approval_narration(\n                request=request,\n                bead_type=e.bead_type,\n                validation_result=e.approval_card.get(\"validation_result\", {}),\n            )\n\n            # Broadcast approval_required event to canvas\n            broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n            await broadcaster.broadcast(\n                SSEEvent(\n                    event_type=EventType.APPROVAL_REQUIRED,\n                    data={\n                        \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n                        \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n                        \"approval_id\": approval_id,\n                        \"approval_card\": e.approval_card,\n                        \"bead_body\": e.bead_body,\n                        \"bead_type\": e.bead_type,\n                        \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n                        \"narration\": approval_narration,  # For voice mode\n                    },\n                    target_session_id=request.session_id,\n                )\n            )\n            logger.info(f\"Broadcast approval_required event for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n            return EscalateResult(\n                bead_id=\"\",  # No bead created yet\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                pending_card=e.approval_card,\n                status=\"awaiting_approval\",\n            )\n        except ValidationRetryExhaustedError as e:\n            # Re-formulation failed - return clarification card\n            logger.warning(f\"Re-formulation exhausted for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n            # Update intent status\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                status=\"needs_clarification\",\n            )\n\n            # Build clarification card\n            # We need to get the original bead body that was formulated before validation\n            # Since we don't have the original bead body at this point, we use the utterance\n            clarification_card = self.build_clarification_card(\n                request=request,\n                original_bead_body=request.utterance,  # Fallback to utterance\n                violations=e.original_violations,\n            )\n\n            # Broadcast clarification_card event to canvas\n            broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n            await broadcaster.broadcast(\n                SSEEvent(\n                    event_type=EventType.CLARIFICATION_CARD,\n                    data={\n                        \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n                        \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n                        \"clarification_card\": clarification_card,\n                        \"violations\": [v.to_dict() if hasattr(v, 'to_dict') else {\"message\": str(v)} for v in e.original_violations],\n                        \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n                    },\n                    target_session_id=request.session_id,\n                )\n            )\n            logger.info(f\"Broadcast clarification_card event for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n            return EscalateResult(\n                bead_id=\"\",  # No bead created\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                pending_card=clarification_card,\n                status=\"needs_clarification\",\n            )\n        except EscalateError:\n            raise\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Escalate failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            raise EscalateError(f\"Escalate failed: {e}\") from e\n\n\n# Global escalate handler instance\n_handler: Optional[EscalateHandler] = None\n\n\ndef get_escalate_handler(store=None) -> EscalateHandler:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global escalate handler instance.\"\"\"\n    global _handler\n    if _handler is None:\n        _handler = EscalateHandler(store=store)\n    return _handler\n\n\nasync def escalate_intent(request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n    \"\"\"\n    Convenience function to escalate an intent.\n\n    Args:\n        request: The escalate request\n\n    Returns:\n        EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card\n    \"\"\"\n    handler = get_escalate_handler()\n    return await handler.escalate_intent(request)\n\n\nasync def handle_terminal_failure(\n    intent_id: str,\n    session_id: str,\n    topic_id: str | None,\n    failure_reason: str,\n    error_type: str = \"unknown\",\n    bead_ref: str | None = None,\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Handle a terminal failure for an intent.\n\n    Plan §10 The Async Path: terminal failure handling.\n    - Sets intents.status = 'failed'\n    - Stores failure reason in bead_watch.last_refusal_reason (if bead exists)\n    - Creates failed card in session store\n    - Broadcasts task_failed SSE event\n\n    Args:\n        intent_id: The intent that failed\n        session_id: The session ID\n        topic_id: The topic ID (optional)\n        failure_reason: Human-readable failure reason\n        error_type: Type of error (e.g., \"worker_crash\", \"invalid_input\")\n        bead_ref: Associated bead reference (optional)\n    \"\"\"\n    from ..session.store import get_store as get_session_store\n    from ..sse.broadcaster import get_broadcaster, SSEEvent, EventType\n\n    store = get_session_store()\n\n    logger.info(f\"Handling terminal failure for intent {intent_id}: {failure_reason}\")\n\n    # Step 1: Update intent status to 'failed'\n    await store.update_intent_status(intent_id, \"failed\")\n    logger.info(f\"Set intent {intent_id} to failed status\")\n\n    # Step 2: Store failure reason in bead_watch if bead exists\n    if bead_ref:\n        try:\n            await store.update_bead_watch_refusal(\n                bead_ref=bead_ref,\n                refusal_reason=failure_reason,\n                comment_index=-1,  # No comment index for terminal failures\n                refusal_count_add=1,\n            )\n            logger.info(f\"Stored failure reason for bead {bead_ref}\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to update bead_watch for {bead_ref}: {e}\")\n\n    # Step 3: Create or find topic for failed card\n    # Fetch intent once for both topic creation and result_type derivation\n    intent = None\n    try:\n        intent = await store.get_intent(intent_id)\n    except Exception as e:\n        logger.warning(f\"Failed to fetch intent for result_type derivation: {e}\")\n\n    final_topic_id = topic_id\n    if not final_topic_id and intent:\n        utterance_id =       db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n                    async with db.execute(\n                        \"SELECT raw_text FROM utterances WHERE id = ?\",\n                        (utterance_id,)\n                    ) as cursor:\n                        utterance_row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                        if utterance_row:\n                            utterance_text = utterance_row[\"raw_text\"][:80]\n                            final_topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                                label=f\"Failed: {utterance_text}\",\n                                session_id=session_id,\n                                topic_type=\"exception\",\n                            )\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.warning(f\"Failed to create topic for failed card: {e}\")\n\n    # Step 4: Link intent to topic and create failed card\n    if final_topic_id:\n        try:\n            # Link intent to topic final_topic_id)\n\n            # Create failed result card\n            summary = f\"Task Failed: {error_type.replace('_', ' ').title()}\"\n            data = {\n                \"bead_ref\": bead_ref,\n                \"failure_reason\": failure_reason,\n                \"error_type\": error_type,\n                \"message\": f\"Task failed: {failure_reason}\",\n                \"action_hint\": \"This task encountered a terminal error and cannot proceed. Review the error details and retry if applicable.\",\n            }\n\n            # Derive result_type from intent data\n            result_type = derive_result_type(\n                intent_type=intent.get(\"intent_type\") if intent else None,\n                project_slug=intent.get(\"project_slug\") if intent else None,\n                lookup_kind=intent.get(\"lookup_kind\") if intent else None,\n            )\n\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=intent_id,\n                topic_id=final_topic_id,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                summary=summary,\n                data=data,\n                urgency=\"high\",\n                result_type=result_type,\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created failed card {result_id} for intent {intent_id}\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to create failed card for intent {intent_id}: {e}\")\n\n    # Step 5: Broadcast task_failed event via SSE\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n    await broadcaster.broadcast(\n        SSEEvent(\n            event_type=EventType.TASK_FAILED,\n            data={\n                \"bead_id\": bead_ref,\n                \"intent_id\": intent_id,\n                \"session_id\": session_id,\n                \"topic_id\": final_topic_id,\n                \"failure_reason\": failure_reason,\n                \"error_type\": error_type,\n                \"message\": f\"Task failed: {failure_reason}\",\n                \"timestamp\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n            },\n            target_session_id=session_id,\n        )\n    )\n\n    logger.info(f\"Broadcast task_failed event for intent {intent_id}\")\n","numLines":1421,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1421}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_298c1720f1e74e1e9655f4a6","type":"tool_result","content":"260\t\n261\t**Pending/ack card render path.** The pending/ack card is **not** a component-library render: no `results` row exists until bead closure, so there is nothing for `results.result_type` selection to key on and no `result_id` for `card_cache`. Pending/ack cards — including per-source fetch-progress and elapsed-time states — are built-in frontend templates shipped in `src/canvas/`, exactly like the welcome and generic fallback cards (see Component Library → Built-in cards). The pending lifecycle starts client-side, not with a server event: at submit time the canvas creates a **local per-utterance pending placeholder** — before any server response has arrived, so even a hung server leaves a card on canvas to age — which splits into per-thread pending cards when the dispatch ack arrives. The client fills those from SSE events (dispatch ack, per-source progress, bead ref on `bf create` return) and replaces them with the component-rendered card when the real result lands; the 30s aged-pending timer runs from the local placeholder's creation, not from any SSE event (see the Degraded-State UX aged-pending row).\n262\t\n263\tThe bead watcher subsequently bridges bead closure to result delivery.\n264\t\n265\t#### Beads-Workspace Scoping\n266\t\n267\t\"The appropriate project\" is deliberately **not** another repo's `.beads/`. Every aide-de-camp-originated bead lives in the aide-de-camp repo's own beads workspace, carrying its target project as the `--project {slug}` field. One workspace for create, poll, and fence means the escalate strand and the bead watcher run every `bf` invocation from the aide-de-camp checkout directory and are guaranteed to see every bead they own; NEEDLE workers roam across workspaces and pick these up like any other.\n268\t\n269\tThe one exception is read-only: the fetch matrix's per-project bead listing reports on a project's *own* backlog, so it runs `bf list --status open` in that project's repo checkout when the registry entry has a `repo_path` with a `.beads/` workspace — deliberately with **no** `--project` filter: a project's own workspace doesn't tag its beads with its aide-de-camp slug, so `--project {slug}` there would return zero or near-zero rows and render a plausible-looking empty backlog on camera. The `--project {slug}` filter belongs only to the fallback: when there is no local workspace, fetch falls back to the aide-de-camp workspace filtered by `--project {slug}` — still real data, but only aide-de-camp-originated beads — and `fetch_coverage` records the narrower scope so the card carries a caveat (\"no local beads workspace for {slug}; showing aide-de-camp-originated beads only\").\n270\t\n271\t#### Generated-Bead Safety\n272\t\n273\tAn LLM-authored bead body is an instruction that autonomous workers will execute. Unconstrained, this is the system's sharpest edge — live evidence: an escalate-authored bead containing an unscoped `kubectl delete pod` instruction put NEEDLE workers into a refusal loop (workers correctly refuse it; NEEDLE re-dispatches it forever). Every escalate-authored bead body therefore passes a deterministic validation gate before `bf create`:\n274\t\n275\t- **Cluster-mutation verbs are denied.** Bead bodies must not instruct workers to run `kubectl apply/create/delete/scale/patch/edit/annotate/rollout` or any other live mutation of a managed resource. These violate the GitOps rule and workers refuse them.\n276\t- **Mutations must be phrased as declarative-config edits.** Any desired change to cluster state is written as \"edit the manifest under `k8s/<cluster>/...` in `jedarden/declarative-config`, commit, push; ArgoCD syncs\" — the only sanctioned mutation path (see Action Execution Model).\n277\t- **Scoping is required.** Bead bodies that reference cluster resources must name the cluster, namespace, and specific resource. Unscoped operational instructions (\"delete the pod\", \"restart everything\") are rejected.\n278\t\n279\tValidation failure → the bead is not created; the escalate strand re-formulates once with the failure reason in context, and surfaces a clarification card if the second attempt also fails."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"7d23932f-ca36-4709-b3a7-8f346939331b","uuid":"528d28c6-a63e-488a-a996-0c00018d5d6d","timestamp":"2026-07-23T23:35:10.445Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/docs/plan/plan.md","content":"\n**Pending/ack card render path.** The pending/ack card is **not** a component-library render: no `results` row exists until bead closure, so there is nothing for `results.result_type` selection to key on and no `result_id` for `card_cache`. Pending/ack cards — including per-source fetch-progress and elapsed-time states — are built-in frontend templates shipped in `src/canvas/`, exactly like the welcome and generic fallback cards (see Component Library → Built-in cards). The pending lifecycle starts client-side, not with a server event: at submit time the canvas creates a **local per-utterance pending placeholder** — before any server response has arrived, so even a hung server leaves a card on canvas to age — which splits into per-thread pending cards when the dispatch ack arrives. The client fills those from SSE events (dispatch ack, per-source progress, bead ref on `bf create` return) and replaces them with the component-rendered card when the real result lands; the 30s aged-pending timer runs from the local placeholder's creation, not from any SSE event (see the Degraded-State UX aged-pending row).\n\nThe bead watcher subsequently bridges bead closure to result delivery.\n\n#### Beads-Workspace Scoping\n\n\"The appropriate project\" is deliberately **not** another repo's `.beads/`. Every aide-de-camp-originated bead lives in the aide-de-camp repo's own beads workspace, carrying its target project as the `--project {slug}` field. One workspace for create, poll, and fence means the escalate strand and the bead watcher run every `bf` invocation from the aide-de-camp checkout directory and are guaranteed to see every bead they own; NEEDLE workers roam across workspaces and pick these up like any other.\n\nThe one exception is read-only: the fetch matrix's per-project bead listing reports on a project's *own* backlog, so it runs `bf list --status open` in that project's repo checkout when the registry entry has a `repo_path` with a `.beads/` workspace — deliberately with **no** `--project` filter: a project's own workspace doesn't tag its beads with its aide-de-camp slug, so `--project {slug}` there would return zero or near-zero rows and render a plausible-looking empty backlog on camera. The `--project {slug}` filter belongs only to the fallback: when there is no local workspace, fetch falls back to the aide-de-camp workspace filtered by `--project {slug}` — still real data, but only aide-de-camp-originated beads — and `fetch_coverage` records the narrower scope so the card carries a caveat (\"no local beads workspace for {slug}; showing aide-de-camp-originated beads only\").\n\n#### Generated-Bead Safety\n\nAn LLM-authored bead body is an instruction that autonomous workers will execute. Unconstrained, this is the system's sharpest edge — live evidence: an escalate-authored bead containing an unscoped `kubectl delete pod` instruction put NEEDLE workers into a refusal loop (workers correctly refuse it; NEEDLE re-dispatches it forever). Every escalate-authored bead body therefore passes a deterministic validation gate before `bf create`:\n\n- **Cluster-mutation verbs are denied.** Bead bodies must not instruct workers to run `kubectl apply/create/delete/scale/patch/edit/annotate/rollout` or any other live mutation of a managed resource. These violate the GitOps rule and workers refuse them.\n- **Mutations must be phrased as declarative-config edits.** Any desired change to cluster state is written as \"edit the manifest under `k8s/<cluster>/...` in `jedarden/declarative-config`, commit, push; ArgoCD syncs\" — the only sanctioned mutation path (see Action Execution Model).\n- **Scoping is required.** Bead bodies that reference cluster resources must name the cluster, namespace, and specific resource. Unscoped operational instructions (\"delete the pod\", \"restart everything\") are rejected.\n\nValidation failure → the bead is not created; the escalate strand re-formulates once with the failure reason in context, and surfaces a clarification card if the second attempt also fails.","numLines":20,"startLine":260,"totalLines":1213}}}

The take is **canvas-only**: utterances enter through the canvas page's Web Speech STT — the Realtime API voice session (Phase 4's audio surface) is not part of the launch recording; a voice-session demo is post-launch work (see Open Question 6). Every utterance uses a registered project (pbx-web, whisper-stt) and only intents the registry supports for that project — step 5's `task-profile` included: both example entries list it in `intent_support`. The `action` intent is deliberately excluded from the script — its execution model carries its own constraints and adds risk without adding demo value.\n956\t\n957\t**Note:** Both scripted projects (pbx-web, whisper-stt) are hosted on ardenone-cluster, whose ArgoCD applications live on ardenone-manager and are readable via the existing no-auth read-only proxy at `https://argocd-ro-ardenone-manager-ts.ardenone.com:8444`. This ArgoCD-readability path (Option B, per HUMAN decision bead adc-359d) ensures the scripted status cards render without `fetch_coverage` caveat strips, satisfying smooth criterion 3.\n958\t\n959\t| Step | Utterance | Intent(s) | Expected canvas output |\n960\t|------|-----------|-----------|------------------------|\n961\t| 1 | \"Has the pbx web caught up, and what's the state of whisper stt?\" | status ×2 (pbx-web, whisper-stt) | Router splits into two threads; two cards appear and resolve **in parallel** — pod status, ArgoCD sync state, recent commits (from declarative-config, where both apps' manifests live), per-project status. Both projects are on ardenone-cluster; their ArgoCD apps (pbx-web, whisper-stt) resolve via the ardenone-manager read-only proxy, so no `fetch_coverage` caveat appears. |\n962\t| 2 | \"Pull up the recent logs for whisper stt.\" | lookup/logs (whisper-stt) | Log-lookup card (`lookup:logs:whisper-stt`) with recent log lines, appearing under the existing whisper-stt topic as its own card beside the step-1 status card — one card per (topic, result_type): grouped, not a new pile |\n963\t| 3 | \"Should pbx web keep using the static site generator, or is it time to move to a dynamic frontend? Bead **closure is not part of the take** — the pending card is the demo-visible outcome |\n966\t| 6 | \"Anything new on pbx web since we started?\" | status (pbx-web) | Existing pbx-web **status** card updates **in place** — no new card — with a diff summary naming exactly what changed since step 1. The diff runs against the step-1 status result via `previous_result_id`, which is scoped to the same result_type on the same topic: step 3's brainstorm card is a different result_type, stays on canvas untouched, and never enters the diff. The diff strip renders here because step 1's result is in-session — the same session-scoped display rule that suppresses \"changes since the seed run\" strips on step 1 (see Cold start & demo seed — Topic scope vs. session scope). If nothing changed, an explicit \"no changes since\" diff state (also a passing outcome). No speed contrast is claimed: seeding warms both scripted topics, so step 1 is just as warm |\n967\t\n968\t#### Definition of \"smooth\" (measurable)\n969\t\n970\tThe take passes only if all of the following hold — each is observable in the recording or in the timing log:\n971\t\n972\t1. First partial card ≤ 3s after end of utterance, on **every** scripted step — for the task-profile step (5) the qualifying card is the ack/pending card rendered on router completion, per the Latency Budget's task-profile note (this finally verifies the plan's own hot-path target on camera)\n973\t2. Every thread from a multi-intent utterance renders as its own card — zero dropped or merged threads\n974\t3. Zero visible error states: no raw JSON, no stack traces, no empty cards, no failed-fetch caveats on scripted topics — and scope caveats count as failures here, explicitly: the Beads-Workspace Scoping fallback's \"showing aide-de-camp-originated beads only\" strip on a scripted card fails the take (the seeding runbook's `repo_path` verification exists to make it impossible)\n975\t4. Zero dead-end cards: every card either resolves with data or shows an honest pending state the script accounts for (step 5)\n976\t5. The SSE connection never visibly drops and the page is never refreshed during the take\n977\t6. STT accepts each scripted utterance on the first attempt (a mis-transcription restarts the take, it is not edited around)\n978\t7. The full take completes in a single unedited capture\n979\t\n980\t#### Known-issues register\n981\t\n982\tSeeded from what this plan already establishes. Script waits for neither closure nor the breaker |\n989\t| Degraded-state error cards are fully specified in Degraded-State UX but not yet implemented or verified against real source timeouts | Degraded-State UX (card templates); Open Question 2 (timeout behavior unmeasured) | **Yes** | Implement and verify the fixed card templates; a mid-take kubectl/ArgoCD timeout must render as the designed caveat strip on an otherwise-complete card, not as an error or a blank |\n990\t| Clarification round-trip flow unresolved | Open Question 7 | No | Script uses exact registry aliases so clarification never triggers; the no-match card (below) is the insurance if it does |\n991\t| Both scripted projects NOW declare `cluster: ardenone-cluster`, whose ArgoCD applications live on ardenone-manager — and ardenone-manager HAS a no-auth read-only ArgoCD proxy (`https://argocd-ro-ardenone-manager-ts.ardenone.com:8444`) that the fetch strand can consume directly.
